source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
A spherically symmetric gravitational system of particles has a mass density
$\rho = \begin{cases} 
\rho_0 & \text{for } r \leq R \\
0 & \text{for } r > R
\end{cases}$

where $\rho_0$ is a constant. A test mass can undergo circular motion under the influence of the gravitational field of particles. Its speed $V$ as a function of distance $r$ $(0 < r < \infty)$ from the centre of the system is represented by
<image_1>
extraction_notes: 题目通过<image_1>引用图像，且答案依赖图像中的图表/选项。
options字段只有(A)(B)(C)(D)标签，没有可映射的选项文本。
image_1字段是PIL.Image对象描述，不是图片路径或URL，因此image_paths留空。

issues: Option text is not provided; only choice labels/count information is available.

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says the speed function is 'represented by the graph in the image'
the task appears to require selecting among visual graph options rather than using text alone
multi_step: question_text gives a piecewise density definition for r<=R and r>R, which suggests reasoning across multiple regimes
the task combines physics interpretation with matching the result to a graph
multiple-choice graph selection typically requires comparing derived qualitative behavior against visual options
verifiability: answer_manifest indicates a discrete choice answer is present
answer_text is a single unambiguous label '(C)'
verifiability is reduced because asset_registry_record notes that option text is not provided; only choice labels/count information is available

risk_flags: Correctness depends on interpreting graph options in the image.
Option text is not provided; only choice labels/count information is available.

decision_reasons: Question text, primary image, and a discrete answer label are all present, so the core problem is still recoverable.
The item is strongly image-dependent, and the required graph options appear to rely on interpreting the image rather than text alone.
Option text is not separately provided, which reduces verifiability and makes the sample less complete than a clean keep candidate.
Because the sample retains usable core semantics but has a notable asset gap, low_priority is preferred over reject.
